data.title: European Lithium Cancels 30 Million Performance Rights by Agreement
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

European Lithium ( (AU:EUR) ) just unveiled an update.

European Lithium Limited has updated the market on changes to its capital structure, reporting the cessation of a class of performance rights listed under the ASX security code EURAJ. The company advised that 30,000,000 of these performance rights were cancelled on 21 April 2026 by mutual agreement between the entity and the holder, reducing the number of such rights on issue.

The cancellation of this substantial block of performance rights may streamline the company’s equity structure and potentially lessen future dilution for existing shareholders. It also signals a renegotiation or conclusion of prior incentive or performance-based arrangements, which could have implications for management incentives and the company’s approach to capital management going forward.

More about European Lithium

European Lithium Limited is an ASX-listed company operating in the lithium sector, focused on the exploration and development of lithium assets to supply the battery and electric vehicle markets. The company’s securities trade under the ticker EUR on the Australian Securities Exchange, reflecting its positioning within the broader battery materials and clean energy value chain.

Average Trading Volume: 8,975,490

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: A$394.7M

data.title: ACME Solar Hits 2 GWh Storage Milestone in Rajasthan, Targets 10 GWh by 2027
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

ACME Solar Holdings Ltd. ( (IN:ACMESOLAR) ) has shared an announcement.

ACME Solar Holdings Ltd. has crossed a significant benchmark by cumulatively operationalising more than 2 GWh of battery energy storage system capacity in Rajasthan, making it one of the first Indian renewable energy players to deploy storage at this scale. The company’s operational battery portfolio in the state now totals 591.18 MW / 2,031.24 MWh across subsidiaries, supporting its plan to reach 10 GWh of storage capacity by 2027.

These battery projects are engineered to charge during off-peak periods and discharge during peak demand, enhancing grid reliability, improving power utilisation, and helping balance supply and demand on the network. The milestone strengthens ACME Solar’s position in India’s fast-growing renewable and storage market, signaling greater operational depth in managing intermittency and providing more stable power for offtakers and the broader grid ecosystem.

More about ACME Solar Holdings Ltd.

ACME Solar Holdings Ltd. is a leading integrated renewable energy company in India with a diversified 8,071 MW portfolio spanning solar, wind, energy storage, firm and dispatchable renewable energy, and hybrid solutions. It operates 2,990 MW of contracted capacity and about 2.0 GWh of battery energy storage, with a further 5,081 MW and roughly 15 GWh of storage under construction under long-term power purchase agreements.

Average Trading Volume: 145,454

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Buy

Current Market Cap: 172.9B INR

FireFly Metals ( (AU:FFM) ) has shared an update.

FireFly Metals has reported that all resolutions put to shareholders at its general meeting on 22 April 2026 were passed by poll with strong majorities. The approvals included an equal capital reduction with an in-specie distribution of shares, as well as ratifications of several prior equity issuances through charity, institutional, Canadian and share purchase plan placements.

The comprehensive endorsement of these resolutions effectively validates FireFly Metals’ recent capital management and fundraising activities. This support strengthens the company’s balance sheet flexibility and confirms shareholder backing for its current growth and financing strategy, which may enhance its ability to advance projects and maintain its dual-listed market presence.

Auric Mining Ltd. ( (AU:AWJ) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Auric Mining Ltd. has lodged an Appendix 3B notice with the ASX outlining a proposed issue of new securities via a placement or similar capital-raising structure. The proposed issuance involves up to five new classes of securities, each with a maximum of 250,000 units, with the issue date targeted for 24 April 2026.

The company has applied for the new securities to be quoted on the ASX once issued, consistent with listing rule requirements for disclosure and quotation. This planned capital raising is expected to provide additional funding flexibility for Auric Mining’s ongoing activities and may influence its capital structure and liquidity in the market.

data.title: Ardiden Options Lapse, Trimming Potential Diluted Share Capital
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Ardiden ( (AU:ADV) ) has provided an announcement.

Ardiden Ltd has notified the market that 930,813 ADV-AQ options, which were due to expire on April 20, 2026 with an exercise price of $0.8385, have lapsed without being exercised. The cessation of these options marginally reduces the company’s potential diluted share capital, clarifying its capital structure for investors and removing these instruments from its outstanding securities on issue.

This administrative announcement signals that no additional shares will be created from this particular tranche of options, which can slightly limit future equity dilution for existing shareholders. While the move does not directly alter Ardiden’s current issued share count, it simplifies the mix of its listed securities and provides updated transparency on the company’s outstanding equity instruments.

More about Ardiden

Ardiden Ltd is an Australia-listed company trading under the ASX code ADV. The brief filing does not specify its industry or primary operations, but indicates it is an established listed entity managing a range of listed securities and options as part of its capital structure.

data.title: NZME Highlights 2025 Resilience, Innovation and Governance Refresh at AGM
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from NZME ( (NZMEF) ).

NZME used its 2026 annual shareholders’ meeting to highlight strong performance across its radio, print and OneRoof divisions in 2025, despite a challenging domestic economic backdrop. The company cited disciplined cost control, innovation in digital products such as the OneRoof app, iHeartCountry and the Herald NOW streaming news service, and a significantly strengthened balance sheet with lower debt, which it says provides greater flexibility for future investment and shareholder returns.

The board also detailed governance changes over the past year, including the appointments of new directors and a new chief financial officer, and confirmed plans for a leadership transition on the audit and risk committee as long-serving director Carol Campbell prepares to retire. Directors emphasized that the refreshed board, combined with NZME’s broad multi-platform audience reach, positions the company to better navigate industry challenges while maintaining its focus on creating value for shareholders.

More about NZME

New Zealand Media and Entertainment (NZME) is a diversified media company operating across radio, print publishing, digital news and real estate platforms in New Zealand. Its core businesses include radio networks, the New Zealand Herald and related digital properties, alongside the OneRoof real estate platform, giving it broad multi-platform reach across news, entertainment and property markets.

data.title: State Street Group Ceases to Be Substantial Holder in Electro Optic Systems
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Electro Optic Systems Holdings Limited ( (AU:EOS) ) has shared an announcement.

State Street Corporation and several of its asset management and custody subsidiaries have filed a notice that they have ceased to be substantial shareholders in Electro Optic Systems Holdings Limited. The change reflects a series of collateral movements, stock lending transactions and transfers involving ordinary shares, which collectively reduced their voting power below the substantial holding threshold, potentially broadening the company’s share register and altering its institutional investor mix.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:EOS) stock is a Buy with a A$12.95 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Electro Optic Systems Holdings Limited stock, see the AU:EOS Stock Forecast page.

Electro Optic Systems Holdings Limited is an Australian-listed company operating in the defence and space technology sector. It develops and supplies advanced electro-optic systems and related technologies, serving defence, aerospace and security markets that rely on sophisticated imaging, tracking and control solutions.

data.title: FirstWave lands high-margin AI compliance deal with Mexico’s Banobras
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

FirstWave Cloud Technology ( (AU:FCT) ) has shared an announcement.

FirstWave Cloud Technology has secured a two-year AI-powered compliance management software subscription contract with Banobras, Mexico’s state-owned national development bank, following a competitive tender. The USD250,000 deal, running from April 2026 to March 2028, is delivered as software with no incremental delivery costs, generating 100% gross margin for FirstWave.

The Banobras win marks the company’s first significant public sector reference for its AI-driven compliance strategy built around Open-AudIT 6 and related modules. It is also the first major deal through channel partner OmniPrinter, linked to conglomerate Grupo Salinas, providing a strategic foothold in the Mexican market and a platform to pursue further opportunities in a compliance-focused environment.

More about FirstWave Cloud Technology

FirstWave Cloud Technology is a global software company founded in 2004 that provides enterprise-grade, AI-powered network management, automation, audit and cybersecurity solutions. Its software is used by more than 150,000 organisations in 178 countries, serving major clients such as Telmex, Telstra, Claro and NASA, and has been expanded through the acquisitions of Opmantek and Saisei Networks.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: A$6.38M

See more insights into FCT stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.
